query: What is the Mass Shooting Tracker (massshootingtracker.site) total number of mass shootings recorded for full-year 2025, and specifically for July 2025? This tracker uses the broad 4+ people shot definition.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line on the Mass Shooting Tracker (massshootingtracker.site) 2025 figures:

**What I found:**
- The tracker uses the broad "4+ people shot" definition and serves its data from an S3 JSON endpoint: `https://mass-shooting-tracker-data.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/2025-data.json`

**The key limitation:** I could **not** verify an exact full-year 2025 total or a clean July 2025 count directly from the tracker. The JSON file is sorted newest-first, and every fetch tool truncated around July 7 (cutting off the first half of the year), while bash/curl was disabled and all CORS proxies/Wayback were blocked.

**Verifiable findings:**
- A direct read returned **316 incident objects covering only ~July 7 → Dec 31, 2025** — a partial count, not the full year. So the tracker's 2025 total is **at least 316** and certainly higher.
- **July 2025 specifically could not be reliably counted** — it fell exactly at the truncation boundary, yielding inconsistent partial reads (~31–47).

**Context from narrower trackers** (not MST): Wikipedia's 2025 list reports **425 shootings** (420 killed, 1,900 wounded); Gun Violence Archive reports **~407–408**. Because MST's "4+ shot" definition is broader, its 2025 total very likely **exceeds 425**, but I couldn't confirm the exact integer.

The precise numbers are retrievable by anyone with shell access via `curl` + `jq 'length'` on the S3 endpoint (and a `date startswith "2025-07"` filter for July).

sources.title: Mass Shooting Tracker (home page, definition)
sources.snippet: We define a 'mass shooting' as a single outburst of violence in which four or more people are shot.

sources.title: Mass Shootings in 2025 (data page, renders client-side)
sources.snippet: Data compiled by an independent all-volunteer group based out of reddit (r/GunsAreCool).

sources.title: 2025-data.json (S3 data endpoint, sorted newest-first)
sources.snippet: JSON array of incident objects; partial read returned 316 objects from Dec 31 back to ~July 7, 2025 before truncation.

sources.title: List of mass shootings in the United States in 2025 - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: A total of 420 people were killed and 1,900 people wounded in 425 shootings.

sources.title: Gun Violence By The Numbers in 2025 - The Trace
sources.snippet: At least 40,000 people were shot in 2025; shooting deaths and injuries fell to some of the lowest levels on record.

sources.title: 2025 Active Shooter Statistics: A Year in Review
sources.snippet: Per Gun Violence Archive there were around 407 mass shootings in the United States in 2025.

sources.title: GitHub - MassShootingTracker/shootingtracker
sources.snippet: Mass Shooting Tracker website repository (README only; no 2025 data files exposed).

Wikipedia 2025 US mass shootings total (4+ victims, narrower than MST)
Gun Violence Archive 2025 mass shootings (separate tracker, context)
